NTSB briefing findings from the Air Canada Express LaGuardia incident may reveal systemic factors affecting regional carrier operations and safety protocols at major hub airports.

Drones Mar 24, 2026

DJI’s latest deals make shaky phone footage a thing of the past

Smartphones today can shoot cinematic 4K, handle low light, and even mimic DSLR depth. But there’s still one thing they can’t fix on their own: movement. That tiny shake when you walk. That awkward jitter when you pan. That “almost good” clip that never quite looks professional. That’s exactly wh...

DJI's stabilization technology addresses the final gap between smartphone and professional video production by eliminating movement artifacts that software alone cannot correct.

See DroneDJ →

Commercial Mar 24, 2026

Why Airlines Are Retiring the 757

Few aircraft in commercial aviation have earned the kind of reputation the Boeing 757 built over the years. It was powerful, versatile, and capable of flying routes that many narrowbody aircraft simply couldn’t handle. Airlines loved it because it could operate from short runways, climb quickly t...

The 757's retirement reflects airlines' shift toward higher-capacity twins and modern fuel efficiency over the aircraft's unique operational flexibility that once justified its premium operating costs.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Mar 24, 2026

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.

Modern SMS shifts aviation from accident investigation to predictive risk identification, fundamentally changing how operators prevent incidents before they occur.

Unifly's acquisition of EuroUSC-Benelux consolidates UTM infrastructure across Northern Europe, reducing fragmentation in drone airspace management.

Military Mar 24, 2026

Fusion engine for missile warning lacks a government dataset

WASHINGTON – National missile warning and tracking could be improved if all government agencies shared the raw data they gather. “There are agencies that have sensors that can provide data that would be very supplemental to the missile-defense mission, but they designed it for a completely differ...

Fragmented government sensor data collection hampers national missile defense effectiveness, suggesting procurement strategies must prioritize interagency data-sharing infrastructure alongside hardware development.

How Much Does It Cost To Upgrade To Premium Economy On US Airlines?

A closer look at the true cost of upgrading to premium economy on major US carriers.

Premium economy upgrade costs vary dramatically across carriers, ranging from $50-$500+ depending on route length and booking timing, making strategic upgrade timing crucial for cost-conscious business travelers.

Commercial Mar 24, 2026

Airbus Snub: Why Finnair Just Chose Embraer's E2 Over The A220

Finnair's bold move to order 18 Embraer E195-E2 jets and 12 used Airbus A320/A321s signals a major shift in the airline's short-haul strategy.

Finnair's preference for Embraer's newer E2 family over Airbus's modern A220 suggests regional carriers now prioritize used widebody economics and Embraer's cabin flexibility over traditional narrowbody competition.

See Simple Flying →

Drones Mar 24, 2026

DJI vs Insta360 drone patent war just got a lot more intense

The world may be watching geopolitical tensions rise, but in the world of drone technology, a different kind of war is breaking out. Following DJI’s lawsuit over disputed drone patents, Insta360’s founder has come out swinging — and his response makes one thing clear: this fight is about far more...

Insta360's escalating patent countersuit signals that drone manufacturer competition is shifting from product innovation to intellectual property disputes, potentially affecting development timelines and market consolidation.

Eastern Iowa Airport's diverse traffic mix from trainers to air carriers positions it as a critical regional hub balancing general aviation training with commercial operations.

DJI sues Insta360 days before Avata 360 drone launch

Just as DJI is gearing up to unveil its first-ever 360-degree FPV drone, the Avata 360, the company has launched a legal offensive against its fastest-rising rival, Insta360. And this time, the battle isn’t about specs or features — it’s about who owns the underlying technology powering the next ...

DJI's legal action against Insta360 signals intensifying IP battles in the consumer FPV drone market as manufacturers race to commercialize 360-degree technology.

Tropical Cyclone Narelle Crosses Australia

The powerful storm lashed the northern edge of the continent with damaging winds and drenching rain as it made landfall multiple times.

Cyclone Narelle's multiple landfall patterns create unpredictable wind shear and severe weather challenges for northern Australian airports and en-route operations.

Brussels Airlines' aging A330 fleet with inconsistent business class products reflects the operational challenges of serving niche markets while competing within a major airline group's network strategy.

ANELLO Photonics and Q-CTRL Announce Strategic Collaboration to Redefine Resilient Navigation for UAV’s in GPS-Denied Environments

ANELLO Photonics, the creator of the Silicon Photonics Optical Gyroscope (SiPhOG™) and a leader in

Silicon photonics-based gyroscopes combined with quantum control algorithms could enable autonomous drone navigation without GPS, opening operations in underground, indoor, and electronic-warfare environments where traditional systems fail.

Safety Mar 24, 2026

NTSB Team Still En Route To LaGuardia: Delayed By TSA Lines

The NTSB is undertaking a complete walking inspection of the LaGuardia crash site, facing a tremendous amount of debris strewn across the runway and taxiways.

TSA checkpoint delays hindered NTSB's immediate response to a major crash investigation, highlighting operational friction between airport security and accident investigation priorities.

Industry Mar 24, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational efficiency gains across integrated airline-airport-airspace systems offer near-term emissions reductions before fuel technology transitions reach commercial viability.

See MDPI →

Military Mar 24, 2026

The B-2 Spirit Bomber’s Flat Architecture Explained: Radar Evasion Tactics For Pilots

Inside America's tailless, flying wing stealth bomber.

The B-2's flying-wing design minimizes radar cross-section by eliminating vertical surfaces that reflect electromagnetic waves, fundamentally changing how pilots must compensate for the absence of traditional control surfaces.

Science Through Shadows: How Astronomical Alignments Reveal the Universe

When one celestial object passes in front of another, it can cast a shadow that travels across space – and sometimes across Earth. These moments of alignment, known as eclipses, occultations, and transits, allow scientists to study distant objects in remarkable ways. By observing how light change...

Astronomical observation techniques refined through celestial alignments offer potential applications for spacecraft navigation and Earth observation systems used in aviation operations.
